If you're in the market for a law firm that goes above and beyond—in completely ignoring your instructions, handling confidential information with casual indifference, and treating unauthorized third parties like VIP clients—then look no further, Hage & Hage has mastered this art.

My time with this firm was truly unforgettable, though regrettably for all the wrong reasons. Confidentiality was treated more like a flexible suggestion rather than a professional obligation. Imagine my pleasant surprise when I discovered that detailed information about my sensitive legal matter had been graciously shared with an unauthorized third party—who had simply sent an unsolicited email. Even better, the firm didn't trouble themselves with trivial things like informing me about this correspondence or providing the basic courtesy of copying me. Adding a cherry on top, the firm's representative directly contacted this third party via personal cell phone—quite an exclusive perk I was denied, despite being the actual paying client required to communicate exclusively through their office line.

Communication from this firm was nothing short of spectacularly inadequate. My own direct inquiries frequently went unanswered for days or even weeks, requiring relentless follow-ups to gain minimal acknowledgment. The firm consistently provided short, reluctant responses only after multiple requests. Interestingly enough, however, any unauthorized third-party contact seemed to warrant immediate attention, often receiving prompt, detailed replies within mere hours. Prospective clients might want to consider outsourcing their inquiries to random third parties, as direct client communication evidently ranks extremely low on this firm's priority scale.

Billing was another delightful adventure. While invoices were indeed itemized, the firm adopted a rather innovative practice of not bothering to inform me when my retainer had been depleted. Instead, they surprised me with a thoughtful, unsolicited invoice via email—no prior notice required. Transparent financial communication? Clearly an unnecessary luxury in their esteemed business model. Moreover, the final bill was astronomical, especially considering the minimal activity and lack of tangible results achieved on my behalf. Adding to the confusion, the math on the invoice didn’t even add up correctly; for example, one invoice cited 7 hours at $300 per hour yet somehow totaled $2,310—unless basic arithmetic has recently changed, this discrepancy certainly speaks volumes about their attention to detail.

As a final touch, it's worth noting that when I kindly offered a simple bottle of bourbon as a gesture of appreciation upon the successful completion of my case, I received an immediate and rather stern lecture about ethics and professional codes of conduct. Curiously, these unwavering ethical standards did not seem to extend to their handling of my confidential information or their communications with unauthorized third parties. Selective application of ethical rigor—another unique specialty of this esteemed firm.

In conclusion, if your ideal legal representation includes ignored instructions, confidentiality breaches, superior treatment of unauthorized third parties, delayed responses, unexpected financial surprises, and inexplicable billing discrepancies, congratulations—Hage & Hage is truly your dream firm. For everyone else, I recommend seeking legal assistance elsewhere to preserve your sanity, finances, and professional dignity.

reviews.text: This firm originally reached out to me from another attorney, (he must have referred me to them). When I got the call, the assistant attorney came off as very promising & assuring. However, once I met in court with Nicholas Passalacqua (owner) basically told me that I had no time left & a decision must be made that day. Essentially he could not do anything for me, His assistant attorney who had originally reached out to me, had made some promising, reassuring claims. Unfortunately everything I was told, was a lie. I made a decision that day, went back again for sentencing, & took a county year (8-months). I Consistently had my family call each week from September to Late December, so i could speak with my attorney. They finally got back to my family saying they will get back to me after the holidays & into the new year. I had to call Passalacqua from the jail, A different attorney that was female took the call. The problem with this is that she didn’t represent me in court, & knew essentially nothing about the details of my case, took the call & said Nicholas was busy. I was mainly concerned with contacting them for a little reassurance, but I was given absolutely none, & treated like I didn't exist. The first question I asked the attorney was, why did it take so long (4 months) to get ahold of somebody? She replied by essentially blaming my family for giving them the wrong information of where I was being held.. they also claimed that because I hired Passalacqua & Associates, (not just Nicholas) is basically the reason as to why he didn’t take my call from the jail. I filed a grievance against this firm for the fact that they did not represent me to the best of their ability, making false & misleading statements, & mainly failing to communicate effectively with me as I paid them $30,000 (cash). After I filed the grievance on Passalacuqa, they made a withdrawal from representation, due to a conflict of interest, (they claim). I had 1 more hearing to go to & they dropped me before it.. leaving me with a conflict defender. Which I already had originally had at the beginning of this case, (Nicholas blamed this conflict defender for the reason as to why I had to make a decision in court that day). It is sad that I will be taking this firm to court as they refuse to do the right thing, & give me back some of the money I had paid them especially seeing how we didn’t go to trial, & their failure to communicate with me as a paid client. They took full payment for basically half assed work, & that isn’t right. Passalacqua & Associates come off as very professional, unfortunately they do not treat their clients good at all! Very unprofessional & misleading. I would have never filed a grievance, had they got in contact with me! I hope nobody else has to experience this, & I hope this can help the next person that has any legal troubles & is looking for an attorney! Take my review & many others into consideration before hiring this “Law Firm”.

Equally outstanding was his professionalism. No matter how stressful the situation became, he maintained steady communication, provided clear explanations, and ensured I always felt informed and supported. His calm, prepared, and organized approach helped me navigate an emotionally difficult journey with far more confidence.

Lastly, his deep knowledge of custody law was evident in every strategy they developed and every recommendation they made. He anticipated issues before they arose and provided thoughtful, well‑reasoned guidance that always kept the best interests of my family at the forefront.

I am truly grateful for Carl’s dedication, expertise, and advocacy. I would recommend this attorney without hesitation to anyone facing a complex family law matter.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Very difficult to respond to the vicious accusations made by someone that's not even my client. This person received everything that she was entitled to as part of a divorce, but unfortunately that was not enough. When parties ultimately don't have lots of assets to be divided up, everyone's economic life suffers: an unfortunate reality for many divorces. When people live together and rely on two incomes to support one household, $1 goes much further than when you have two incomes each supporting a new household. The economy is a scale.

I take great pride in my reputation and in my integrity. I have never nor will I ever perjured myself or supplied to the court anything that I know to be false and or manipulated. For this person to accuse me I'll falsifying anything is maddening. Unfortunately, there's little I can do to stop crazy people from doing what crazy people do.

As far as not providing copies of documents regarding the sale of the marital residence, all documents were provided to all attorneys so that they could provide those documents to their clients for review. My obligation as always is to my client.

My reality as a matrimonial attorney is that I will never be able to make every one of my clients happy about the result of a divorce. I do not create people's financial situations, nor do I have anything to do with the relationships that parents have with their children. I always attempt to have my clients look at the big picture and realize that a priority issue, when there is children, is making the children the priority and finding a way for dads and moms to work together. Many people get that concept, but unfortunately not all. I have big shoulders and absolutely can take constructive criticism when warranted. To have a non-client attack me, when she was represented by a very good attorney, leaves me at a loss.

If you never had the "pleasure" of suddenly being served Divorce papers and you find yourself surprised, a bit lost, maybe locked out of your house, confused, in understandable shock, suddenly without money and blind-sided, I strongly recommend you go to M Kalil as soon as possible. He is very experienced and often has helped first-timers. Kalil is pragmatic and logical formost, saves you from MUCH extraneous nonsense by getting to the point and helping one navigate an impossible situation efficiently and to your benefit. The initial consultation is without charge but, better still, his experience and long-standing relationships with other lawyers and judges and local Courts makes your retainer fee go farther by avoiding legal dead ends and wasteful spending. Every dollar invested in his office saves you two more by avoiding unnecessary, wasteful motions and he is mission-focused on your best ultimate outcome. He will not hold your hand or wipe your tears (trust me, you don't want that kind of lawyer), but he will adopt your case in more of a feduciary sense with your solvency and goals as his goals. If you wish simply to resolve your legal Divorce issues and end up in the best position, use him. He's really good at it. His staff is experienced and has been working for him many years (ESPECIALLY Anne), his staff is top notch. If you are understandably sad or shook, ask him to recommend a therapist maybe, but if  you wish simply to get your best outcome financially without nonsense or false leads, get him on your team soonest, please. In my experience, Kalil is an expert on mitigation and obtaining the best result for you personally in an impossible, terrible situation. Divorce produces NO winners, ever, but Kalil will find your path to resolution and place you in the least-bad situation for your benefit, blaze a path for you to stay solvent and hasten your best outcome so you can rebuild your life well. Again, one dollar spent with Kalil saves you many more by avoiding needless pitfalls and costly dead-ends with less experienced other local lawyers - I actually DID check them all out. I cannot recommend him enough. This, from a skeptical first time divorcee, University graduate, professional, NOT a lawyer-fan in general. Name is Anthony, and you're welcome.

reviews.ownerResponse: David.  I take my reviews to heart as this is my business.  I make every attempt to be completely professional and do a good job for clients. I've review my calendar as I don't see you as an existing client, and I've checked emails to see if you've ever emailed me.  Don't see anything.  I have no idea who you are.  I'm concluding that you called and spoke to me one the phone about an issue.  I do that for free for anyone that calls.  I'm also concluding that you didn't like any legal analysis I gave to you. He agreed to help us navigate our legal challenges if we were unable to hire an attorney of his caliber in Warren County. I am forever grateful for Mr. Kalil's ability to not sugar-coat reality while being forthcoming, honest, and sympathetic to my current predicament.

I hired them to represent me in a personal injury case, but instead of acting in my best interest, they only seemed to care about their own agenda.

The lawyer assigned to my case was rude, incompetent, and lacked any sort of empathy for my situation. He failed to communicate with me in a timely manner, ignored my concerns, and repeatedly missed important deadlines. Despite my numerous attempts to get answers and receive proper representation, the firm failed to deliver.

To make matters worse, their billing practices were outrageous and seemingly designed to fleece their clients of as much money as possible. They billed for every single phone call, email, and minute of work, even if it had no bearing on my case. The bills I received were inflated and did not seem to reflect the actual work being done.

In addition, the firm was unprofessional in their communication with other parties involved in my case, often causing unnecessary delays and further frustration for me as a client. The firm was also unresponsive to my requests for documentation, such as itemized bills and court filings, making it impossible for me to understand the progress of my case.

I would strongly advise anyone considering Finer & Fanelli to choose a different firm. They lack any sort of ethical standards, take advantage of their clients, and do not provide the level of representation that one would expect from a law firm. I am in the process of filing a complaint with the state bar association and hope to prevent others from having to endure the same experience that I did.
This law firm gives you 110% and more, and isn’t afraid of getting the truth and justice for you.

To say we were disappointed with Stuart Finer and his team is an understatement.

We hired Stuart to represent us on an FSBO housing purchase. Extremely straightforward and shouldn’t have been that difficult as we didn’t even have any realtors in the transaction.  After having to consistently call them to receive any updates, we ended up closing 2 and a half weeks late... on a two month long closing.

Their team was never able to give us a proper answer to our questions about what was happening during the process, and deflected every time with “don’t worry, we are on it”.  Which definitely was not the case.

To top off this long and arduous process, we found out that the seller’s attorney did not correctly write up the deed and left out 9 acres of our property. This should have been caught by Stuart and his team but was likely never even looked at. After bringing this to Stuart’s attention he tried everything in his power to not do any further reconciliation or take responsibility until we threatened legal action against his team.  We closed in November 2020 and just now, 4 months later have this issue corrected.

I wouldn’t trust this team to mow my grass let alone represent us in any legal matter ever again.

I normally don't write reviews but this one I had to.  After asking people I know I was advised not o use Hobika Law Firm.  Going against my better judgement, I tried to.  It's a local firm and I didn't want to drive to Albany.  I contact the law firm and I was asked what I was calling about.  They transfer me to WC department and I leave a nice detailed message for Kelli Boulia and ask that she contact me.  After 3 days and no contact, I go on line and leave all my information and a detailed message about what I was seeking.  A week goes by and no call from the firm.  Fast forward another week, I call the firm again and tell the lady who answers that I have tried multiple times to talk to someone and ask if they are just not taking new cases.  She says they are and puts me through to WC department.  I leave a second detailed message on a Thursday to Kelli Boulia and ask that she contact me.  Fast forward to Tuesday at 3:45 p.m., the next week and still no call back.  I contact the firm again and they secretary takes all my information about my incident and ask me to hold on.  A female gets back on the phone and asks about my incident again.  I tell her again, thinking I'm speaking with the same lady.  She asks about the date of incident and I tell her.  She gets nasty and tells me that I say a different date before.  She then says you told the secretary this date and now your saying this date, which one is it.  I said "I thought I was talking to the same person because you never introduced yourself".  Still the female doesn't introduce herself.  I then ask her who I am speaking to and she says "Kelli".  Kelly continues to be very harsh and rude.  After taking all my information, she tells me that she will be sending me forms to fill out.  It was apparent that Kelli thinks all WC cases and people are a joke and are just looking for a payout.  Unfortunately, some of us actually work our butts off and get injured.  Someone trying to seek advice or speak to an attorney should at least be contacted back.  I went against my better judgement and tried to stay local and this is what I got.

Update 02/10/2025 - it’s been over 5 years and still waiting for the forms to come in the mail. Clearly Kelly is super super super busy. Thank god Alex Dell Law Firm. A much better pick.
